text.title: Vector DB Security — Procedure
text.summary: Five steps: lock auth → TLS → network → encryption → audit + PII process.

step.name: lock-auth
step.action: Enable API key OR OIDC. Disable anonymous endpoint. Rotate any keys present in git history.
step.decision-gate: If anonymous still reachable on public IP — STOP (r1-no-anonymous).

step.name: tls
step.action: Mount cert; enable TLS ≥1.2; redirect plain HTTP listener to 443 OR disable it.
step.decision-gate: If TLS <1.2 — fix; modern compliance requires ≥1.2 (r2-tls-everywhere).

step.name: network
step.action: Bind listener to private subnet; route external traffic via authenticated proxy. Verify with public-IP nmap.

step.name: encryption
step.action: Enable LUKS / EBS encryption on the data volume. For pgvector enable pg_tde or column-level. Verify keys rotate via KMS policy.
step.decision-gate: If keys not rotatable — fix; static keys are audit findings.

step.name: audit-and-pii
step.action: Enable admin audit log; forward to immutable sink. Document PII detection (Presidio), redaction policy, erasure runbook. Test erasure end-to-end on a fake account.
step.output: audit live; PII process documented + tested
step.decision-gate: If erasure test fails or SLA >30d — fix before declaring done (r5-pii-handling-procedure).
